_section: Native imports — basic dispatch and chunk shape

src:
from m import add, square
print(add(square(3), square(4)))

_comment: alias rebinds the local name; extern_table still records the original
src:
from m import add as plus
print(plus(10, 20))

_comment: import the same module twice (different bindings) — both work
src:
from m import f
from m import f as g
print(f(1) + g(2))

_comment: type error from extern surfaces with its message intact
impure native taints memoization but returns a fresh value each call
src:
from m import counter
print(counter())
print(counter())
print(counter())

_comment: heap-allocating native (returns Str) round-trips through the wire

_section: Code modules — basic inlining (each requested fn ends up in chunk.functions)
src:
from u import normalize
print(normalize('  hi  '))

_section: Code modules — transitive deps pulled in automatically
_comment: single-level: report calls label internally
multi-level chain a → b → c → d all inlined
shared helper inlined ONCE despite both a and b depending on it
src:
from m import a, b
print(a())
print(b())

_comment: alias stays on the user-imported name, transitive helper keeps its bare name
src:
from m import report as r
print(r(1))

_comment: external builtin names (print) inside body aren't mistaken for sibling defs
local var inside body doesn't trigger spurious sibling-def inlining

_section: Mixed code + native + integration with other features
src:
from m import add
from u import shout
print(add(shout(2), shout(3)))

_comment: native called from inside a user def — extern_table inherited to nested chunk
imported native used inside a class method passing self.attr
imported function used inside a lambda
src:
from m import double
fn = lambda x: double(x) + 1
print(fn(5))

_comment: imported native inside a comprehension
src:
from m import square
print([square(x) for x in range(4)])

_comment: import + try/except still catches the right error

m.code:
def safe_div(a, b):
    return a / b

_comment: two modules, disjoint helper names — no collision
src:
from a import f
from b import g
print(f(2))
print(g(3))

_section: Resolver and parser-time errors (with span checks where useful)
_comment: no resolver entry → diagnostic mentions the spec, span covers it exactly
module exists but has no such export → mentions both
code module fails to parse → host's import surfaces the inner error
code module exporting a top-level constant — splicer runs the whole module so K is in scope
module-level effect: pre-built dict bound by name
constant computed from earlier top-level: requires splicer to walk in order
def referencing a top-level constant works at call time
missing export — same diagnostic shape as before
alias on an exported constant
src:
from m import MAX as N
print(N)

_comment: top-level if/else writing a constant — splicer remaps Phi sources

_section: Native typed marshalling — non-i64 wire types round-trip cleanly
_comment: f64 → f64 across the extern dispatch; no coercion to int
bool → bool
src:
from m import negate
print(negate(True))
print(negate(False))

_comment: mixed bool + int args
src:
from m import pick
print(pick(True, 1, 2))
print(pick(False, 1, 2))

_comment: wrong-type arg surfaces the extern's own diagnostic

_section: Namespace import — `import X` materialises a module value
_comment: native module: m.add(...) dispatches via the module's attr table
native module: bare attribute (no call) returns the extern callable
code module: m.k reaches a top-level constant
code module: m.fn(...) calls a top-level def
import alias
missing attr surfaces module name in the error
type(m) is module
every native binding becomes a flat name in scope
code-module star: top-level constants and defs land in scope
imported module's `if __name__ == '__main__':` guard skips, parent's __name__ is still '__main__' afterward
src:
from m import label
print(__name__)
print(label(1))

_comment: module body reads __name__ during splice — sees the spec, not '__main__'

_section: Documented language limitations (regression pins, expected to fail until fixed)
_comment: mutual recursion: inliner pulls both fns in but the parser can't bind forward refs
